setting.note: Icaza's Ecuador is the Andean sierra above and around Quito, where a landowner in debt turns his highland estate over to a foreign oil company and drives the Quechua families tied to it to build a road through mountain and swamp. The map runs from the capital out into the highland countryside toward the eastern cordillera, the range that falls away to the Amazon.

rights.originalWork: In copyright. Jorge Icaza, first published 1934. This book is mapped as a cited work: we store none of its text and reproduce no passages. The places are facts and the notes are our own.
rights.textSource: None stored. A cited book reproduces no text.
rights.territoryNotes: Locations and original commentary only. No reproduction of the copyrighted text, in any territory.
rights.attribution: Huasipungo, Jorge Icaza (1934). Locations mapped by Read the Places; not affiliated with or endorsed by the author or publisher.

palette.note: The red-brown of turned highland earth, the huasipungo soil the workers are bound to. A cited book's colour is not a quotation, because we store no text to quote; it still has to be legible, and this clears WCAG AA on paper.
